mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) indicates continuing labor market expansion, which supports household income and consumption, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Month-over-month increases in PAYEMS coincide with negative 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of observations over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 3% signals restrictive monetary policy that raises discount rates and borrowing costs, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS > 3.0, positive 20-day S&P 500 returns occur in more than 55% of triggered instances over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in both retail sales (RSAFS) and industrial production (INDPRO) signal a broad contraction in aggregate demand and supply, jointly amplifying downside earnings risk and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint declines in RSAFS and INDPRO fail to produce negative 60-day returns in at least 55% of co-declining months over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion regime characterized by unemployment below 5.5%, a month-over-month decline in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signals labor market saturation and impending downturn, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When UNRATE < 5.5% and PAYEMS declines, positive 20-day returns occur in more than 55% of instances over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month drop in retail sales (RSAFS) exceeding 1% signals a sudden consumer spending retrenchment that compresses revenue forecasts, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: RSAFS drops >1% in a month coincide with positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of events.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of a rising federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS month-over-month increase) and a falling consumer price index (CPIAUCSL month-over-month decrease) signals a deflationary monetary tightening that raises real rates sharply, depressing equity valuations and producing negative 20-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Joint occurrence of rising FEDFUNDS and falling CPIAUCSL fails to produce negative 20-day returns in at least 55% of instances over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in industrial production (INDPRO) indicates expanding manufacturing capacity utilization and corporate operating leverage, which drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Month-over-month increases in INDPRO coincide with negative 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of observations over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in housing starts (HOUST) exceeding 5% signals a demand surge in the construction sector that lifts cyclical earnings and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: HOUST increases >5% in a month coincide with negative 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of events.
